questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War.

questions.question: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George Washington served as the first President from 1789 to 1797.

questions.question: In which year did World War I begin?
questions.explanation: World War I began in 1914 after the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Communist Manifesto'?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels co-authored 'The Communist Manifesto' in 1848.

questions.question: What was the name of the ship that brought the Pilgrims to America?
questions.explanation: The Mayflower arrived at Plymouth Rock in 1620.

questions.question: Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 and Chemistry in 1911.

questions.question: In which year did the American Civil War end?
questions.explanation: The Civil War ended in 1865 with the surrender of Confederate forces.

questions.question: What was the name of the ancient Egyptian writing system?
questions.explanation: Hieroglyphics was the formal writing system used in ancient Egypt.

questions.question: Who was the last Tsar of Russia?
questions.explanation: Nicholas II was the last Tsar, abdicating in 1917 during the Russian Revolution.

questions.question: In which year did the French Revolution begin?
questions.explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.
